title: Street Masala Corn

subs.name: canned corn drained well and warmed in a pan with butter for 2 minutes delivers nearly the same result with minimal effort
coconut oil for a dairy-free version; it adds a subtle sweetness that pairs surprisingly well with the spices
a pinch each of cumin powder, coriander powder, and amchur if chaat masala is unavailable

ingredients.name: green chilli (finely chopped, for extra heat)

steps.text: If using fresh corn cobs, shuck them and place in a large saucepan of cold salted water. Bring to a boil and cook for 10–12 minutes until the kernels are tender and sweet but still have a slight snap when bitten. Drain and cool slightly. If using frozen corn kernels, boil in salted water for 3–4 minutes until heated through and tender. Drain well.

If using whole cobs, slice the kernels off the cob with a sharp knife by standing the cob upright and cutting downward close to the cob, rotating as you go. Collect the kernels in a wide bowl. Frozen kernels can go straight into the bowl.

Add the butter to the warm corn kernels and toss until the butter melts and coats every kernel. The heat from the corn should melt the butter without needing to go back on the flame. If using cold corn, warm it in a pan for one minute with the butter over medium heat before proceeding.

Sprinkle chaat masala, red chilli powder, black pepper, and salt over the buttered corn. Toss well to distribute the spices evenly. Squeeze the lemon juice generously over the corn — this is not a garnish but a key ingredient; the acidity brightens all the flavours. Toss one final time.

Serve in paper cups or bowls immediately while the corn is still warm. Scatter finely chopped coriander leaves on top for colour and freshness. Add a pinch of extra chaat masala and a final squeeze of lemon on top. Optional: add finely chopped green chilli for those who want extra heat. Masala corn is best eaten the moment it is assembled — it can sit for up to 10 minutes but the spices and butter settle as it cools.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.

substitution_notes: butter → coconut oil for a dairy-free version; it adds a subtle sweetness that pairs surprisingly well with the spices; curry-powder → a pinch each of cumin powder, coriander powder, and amchur if chaat masala is unavailable; corn → canned corn drained well and warmed in a pan with butter for 2 minutes delivers nearly the same result with minimal effort
